pattern: Glue n to its double and triple, then a single frequency count over the ten possible digits tells you whether the result is a zero-free arrangement of 1 through 9.
hints: Think about what string you'd get by writing n, then 2n, then 3n one after another with nothing in between.
Count how many times each digit 0-9 shows up across that combined string -- you're looking for a very specific frequency pattern.
It only qualifies when the glued string is exactly 9 digits long, has zero occurrences of '0', and every digit from 1 to 9 shows up exactly once.

edge_inputs.description: a number that satisfies the pandigital property
a number whose glued digits repeat
glued string contains a zero digit, which alone disqualifies it
n, 2n and 3n share obviously repeated digits
a mid-range value to exercise a fresh digit pattern

stress.description: random n values spanning the whole allowed range
